response.justification: Bogdan is a strong match for this role. He brings 15+ years of full-stack experience with direct alignment to the primary stack — TypeScript, Node.js, React, and AWS — and his current role at re:cinq explicitly involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, which directly satisfies the increasingly rare 'AI agent development' requirement. His track record spans high-throughput backend systems, cloud infrastructure (AWS CDK, Kubernetes), NoSQL (MongoDB), and a deep, demonstrated commitment to TDD and software craftsmanship. The main gap is the absence of NestJS from his resume, which is the core framework for this position; his Node.js depth makes it a learnable bridge, but it is a real unknown. Similarly, Kotlin is absent and specific testing frameworks (Vitest, Playwright) are not named, though his testing philosophy is evidently strong.
strengths: Current hands-on AI agent and MCP tooling experience at re:cinq directly addresses the job's explicit AI agent development requirement
15+ years of full-stack delivery across the exact primary stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React, AWS, MongoDB, Docker/Kubernetes) with enterprise-scale production proof
Deep, verifiable testing and craftsmanship culture — TDD advocacy, open-source test frameworks, organized refactoring dojos — aligns with a team that lists Vitest as a primary tool
concerns: NestJS is entirely absent from the resume; it is the core backend framework listed and bridging from plain Node.js to opinionated NestJS patterns carries non-trivial ramp-up risk
Kotlin is not mentioned despite being called out as a secondary backend language requirement; Java appears but only in embedded/tooling contexts, not service development
Named testing frameworks (Jest, Vitest, Playwright) are never cited; testing credibility is strong but stack-specific fluency is unverified
